name: zombie_rider.live_open_field_pressure_mcw
description: Live open-field rider row: one staged hostile rider at bow range records live local-combat pressure, close-pressure withdrawal/repositioning, and bounded log/perf stability.

steps.label: post_load_rider_open_field_settle
steps.expected_visible_fact: staged zombie-rider open-field fixture loads to live map

steps.label: audit_saved_noon_before_rider_open_field
steps.abort_reason: installed save did not prove expected noon turn before live zombie-rider window
steps.expected_visible_fact: saved turn proves local noon before open-field rider pressure window

steps.label: audit_saved_zombie_rider_before_open_field
steps.abort_reason: saved active_monsters metadata did not contain required live-scenario rider footing
steps.expected_visible_fact: one hostile zombie rider starts five tiles north at bow-pressure range

steps.label: toggle_safe_mode_off_before_rider_open_field
steps.expected_visible_fact: safe mode is toggled off so the bounded live rider window is not consumed by warnings

steps.label: advance_rider_open_field_pressure_window
steps.expected_visible_fact: bounded live turn window records bow-pressure planning followed by close-pressure withdrawal-repositioning

steps.label: audit_zombie_rider_open_field_live_plan
steps.abort_reason: same-run artifact lacked bow-pressure and withdrawal/reposition live-plan lines
steps.expected_visible_fact: same-run debug artifact proves live rider local-combat planning reaches bow pressure and close-pressure withdrawal

evidence_contract.claim: a staged live zombie rider at visible bow range uses the monster::plan seam to pressure the player at range and then withdraw/reposition at close pressure instead of static melee dogpile behavior
evidence_contract.load_only_verdict: load proof only / inconclusive for zombie-rider live fun behavior
evidence_contract.writeback_note: No save/writeback gate: this scenario proves bounded live-plan behavior from current-run logs plus saved start-state footing; persistent map mutation is not part of the product claim.
